content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSCameraUsageDescription is generic ("This app needs camera access.") and NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a literal placeholder "TODO" (Info.plist), and no location usage appears anywhere in the codebase.
Pierre: Apple requires purpose strings to explain the specific feature and benefit to the user, not boilerplate — reviewers will reject a "TODO" string outright as it looks unfinished, and the camera string doesn't mention document scanning as seen in ScannerView.swift. Fix by writing "SnapScan uses your camera to scan and digitize documents" and either remove the unused location key entirely (since no CoreLocation code exists) or write a real justification if location is actually used.
